entry.title: Re: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter Get And Put Request?
entry.content: @eric_festinger yes I getting the parameters with get request after that I am sending put request to getting all value this put request takes more time.

entry.title: Technical Issue with Appliance: SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA (Trial Version)
entry.content:
Dear SAP community ,

 

I am reaching out to report ongoing issues with the SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA (Trial Version) appliance. Despite multiple attempts to resolve the issue, the appliance remains in a failed state and is currently unusable. Below are the steps I have taken so far to address the problem:

1. AWS Integration and Security Group Configuration:

• Configured the appropriate security group rules for inbound and outbound traffic (e.g., ports 22, 443, 8080).

• The public IP address of my network has been correctly added to the rules.

2. Connection Testing via SSH and HTTPS:

• I successfully connected to the AWS instance, but the software status in the Cloud Appliance Library continues to show as “Failed”.

3. Recreating the Appliance:

• I terminated and recreated the appliance entirely, but the issue persists.

4. Logs and Diagnostics:

• I attempted to access the logs of the virtual machine but could not identify any specific details regarding the root cause of the failure.

 

Error Message and Additional Details:

• AWS Instance ID: i-06b1dd20d3be57d86

• Appliance Status: Active, but software status failed.

• Instance Public IP Address: 44.223.17.212

• AWS Region: us-east-1

• Logs: No relevant entries or specific indicators visible in the appliance interface or AWS console.

• Error Message in Appliance: “The software deployment failed on at least one virtual machine.

Additionally, I have attempted various configurations, including:

• Modifying the network settings,

• Reviewing AWS configurations,

• Restricting the IP range

Additional Information:

• Trial Version: SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA

• Access through SAP CAL: Yes

• Login Credentials: Registered via SAP Universal ID and AWS Cloud account.

 

Thank you in advance for your support. Please feel free to reach out if you require further details.

 

Kind regards,

Victor

entry.title: Problem updating delivery date (EINDT) and statistical delivery date (SLFDT) in PO badi
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Context:

Customer want's to update 3 fields with specific rules in schedule line  in ME22n. When he modifies  the delivery date (EINDT) he wants to add a certain number to this date, then he wants to cancel the standard redetermination of statiscal delivery date (SLFDT) when delivery date (EINDT is modified at screen. Finally he wants to put a certain value to ELDAT (Goods receipt date).

Problem: 

Method PROCESS_SCHEDULE of badi ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST is where i want to do my code as there are already things done for PO in this BADI. Let's say, the customer modifies delivery date from 10.01.2025 to 12.01.2025 and press ENTER. We want for example replace the 12.01.2025 with a specific rule which will lead to 15.01.2025 for EINDT. As said standard SLFDT is redetermined (note 248349) but we don't want so we set it to it's old value. Then we modifiy ELDAT with some other rule.

At the end of all of this, i check if there are differencies between LS_SCHEDULE_INIT that i loaded before doing anything and another structure LS_SCHEDULE with the possible modications. IF <> then i call the infamous SET_DATA( ls_schedule) and SET_DATA(ls_item) cause if i change the EINDT in schedule, i have also to change it in item...

This SET_DATA method triggers the reprocessing all modified objects (START method called) like SCHEDULE or ITEM and THERE IS my problem! The values that i saved at my first processing are lost when the PROCESS_SCHEDULE is triggered again, maybe because the standard have done some modifications, and i can't find a way to keep all my dermine values saved from on call to another cause values are sometimes not the same as at my first "run" in PROCESS_SCHEDULE. This is a nightmare to handle.

When several items are processed at a time, the nightmare is worse. PROCESS_SCHEDULE handles first ITEM 10 SCEDULE 0001, then ITEM 2 SCEDULE 0001 and if there were modification on one or another schedule, it's reprocessing everything in the same sequence and often wich modifications that i don't wanted...

Anyone faced the same problem? Again, I just wish to set values to EINDT SLFDT and ELDAT and tells the standard to stop messing the values i gave him.

Thanks a lot for your time.

Have a great day!

entry.title: Re: How to send Email Notifications only on the Last Retry Attempt in SAP CI/IS when using AEM
entry.content: Thank you, good detailed information is provided.

entry.title: Re: connect to mssql Database
entry.content:
From the Windows Start Menu type in ODBC Data Sources (64-bit)

Click on Add and select the 64 bit client, which you need to install if it's not there, and name the new DSN the same as your 32 bit DSN. CR will find it and carry one.

Talk to your IT guys on how to get it if you don't have it.

entry.title: Re: Problem updating delivery date (EINDT) and statistical delivery date (SLFDT) in PO badi
entry.content: Have you looked into the methods for retrieving the persistent data?

entry.title: Make keyfigure show no value
entry.content:
Hello community

In a report I want to create a keyfigure that when a price is displayed in the pricing condition, no value should be displayed in another pricing condition. We have managed to set the key figure to zero, but we have not yet found a way to make it completely empty. Does anyone know if this is possible?

entry.title: How to hide deleted contacts in CIC0 Transaction
entry.content: I have a requirement to not to show the contacts which are marked for deletion in CIC0 Tcode.  Suggest if any one have any idea in this

entry.title: Re: Connect PostgreSQL after SSH by CrystalReport
entry.content:
SSH is between the Client and Server, Crystal doesn't care how it connects it just assumes the connection is working.

Contact PostgreSQL on how to configure the Client.

Here are the supported Clients, note CR 2020 is BI 4.3 family:

PostgreSQLPostgreSQL (21)9.6ODBC  PostgreSQL 9.6 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC   PostgreSQL 42.1 JDBC End of Support
10ODBC  PostgreSQL 10 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC   PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC End of Support
11ODBC NewPostgreSQL 11 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC  NewPostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC End of Support
12ODBC BI 4.3 SP01+PostgreSQL 12 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP01+PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC Direct

 

13

ODBC BI 4.3 SP02+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP02+PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC Direct
14ODBC BI 4.3 SP03+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP03+PostgreSQL 42.3 JDBC Direct
15ODBC BI 4.3 SP04+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP04+PostgreSQL 42.3 JDBC Direct

(21) Function and Procedure are relevant but different objects in PostgreSQL, only Function is supported in Crystal Reports. Functions are listed under "Stored Procedures", e.g., in CR ODBC driver. For more information, see https://me.sap.com/notes/3403784

entry.title: How to use Customer fields in HRESS_CC_GUIBBF_LEAVREQDIALOG
entry.content:
Hi,

     I have created a z version of 'HRESS_CC_GUIBBF_LEAVREQDIALOG' to ZH_HRESS_CC_GUIBBF_LEAVREQDIALOG but when I click send it shows the original dialog screen and not my version. Where do I configure the new screen?

 

Regards,

Vishal Agrawal

entry.title: Re: transport SAC data between prod and dev tenets
entry.content:
Hi @Eliram_Lugassi,

The error "Files already exist and cannot be moved" typically occurs due to the following reasons:

1.Objects with the same ID already exist in the DEV tenant.
2.Dependencies, such as dimensions or models, are not handled properly.


To resolve this issue, try importing objects one by one. When selecting an object, hold the Ctrl key to choose individual items. 

For ex.

Import main folder first then 

Model data 

Then story 

 

Give this a try it should work!
If you find this helpful, consider upvoting.
Thank you!

and getting the below error. Could you please help me on resolving this error?

Regards,

Ramesh

entry.title: rpc security model B in sybase 16.0
entry.content:
Dear All
Along with saying hello, I would like to know if it is possible to activate the "rpc security model B" option in sybase 16.0 in SYB_BACKUP,
since "sp_serveroption SYB_BACKUP, "rpc security model B", true" does not work for me and says that it does not find the SYB_BACKUP base.
When querying sp_helpserver if SYB_BACKUP appears.
Pay attention to your comments

entry.title: OData Script API Payload to SAC Custom Widget
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are attempting to compile a customer use case for an ad-hoc SAC import using a combination of the OData Scripting Widget and a Custom Widget.  The custom widget would handle the SAC Import Data POST API call and take the JSON payload based on user selections from the SAC story that are passed first to the OData Scripting Widget.  We created the OData Scripting connection to our S/4HANA On-Premise system as follows:

We then leverage the standard getEntitiesFromEntitySet capability and pass in a filter which is derived from some user selections in a helper function. I am storing in a variable ‘ret’ for further processing.

I can observe my JSON payload from the OData getEntitiesfromEntitySet as shown below – this is working fine.

But, I am currently stuck, as the limited JavaScript for Custom Widgets does not seems to be able to recognize this dynamic array object as a function argument input. Would the community have any hints to how I could pass this payload into a custom widget for further processing as a function argument? It’s like it does not recognize the OData payload as an object it can work with. Has anyone successfully done something like this?

"DynamicTypes.QCF410SEGALLOC.ODataResults.entitySetZBPC_SEG_ALLOCArray" to "Unknown Type"

entry.title: What to consider when selling self-developed Fiori applications?
entry.content:
I am an experienced full stack developer working as an employee with large S/4HANA systems. I developed an fiori app (including a RAP backend) that i want to sell to different companies. It will run on S/4HANA On-Premise and Private Cloud. I don´t want to use the SAP Store but instead promote it via my network and sell it directly.

What do i need to consider when doing so?

As far as I understand it, selling a self-built Fiori app is not really different from selling development services. In both cases, SAP technologies (SAPUI5, RAP, ABAP, ...) are being used and the application always runs on customer systems which are licensed SAP systems.

I already got in contact with the SAP Partner Support, but they weren´t able to give me a clear answer. They only gave information regarding the partner program, but not if it is actually necessary in my case or what options i have as a one-person company.

Agradecemos

entry.title: Migrating SAP HANA Cloud Calc Views to SAP Data Sphere
entry.content:
We have a landscape like ECC > SLT > HANA Cloud (CALC View) > SAC.

We have numerous CALC Views which are designed in SAP BAS on HANA Cloud using ECC replicated tables through SLT. Now, we want to migrate all these HANA CALC views to SAP Data Sphere what is a best approach ?

entry.title: Re: NetWeaver RFC SDK: moving from Python to C
entry.content:
Hi,

It's indeed quite unfortunate of SAP to have withdrawn support, as support is crucial in a business environment...

Is there any information yet about whether there's a group that will take over the development of this project?

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud certifications updates and Stay Certified programs releases
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I'm Charis from SAP Product & Solutions learning and I am glad to announce the updates of SAP Commerce Cloud Business Associate and SAP Commerce Cloud Professional certification exams, and the release of their corresponding Stay Certified programs. In this blog post I'm going to provide you a short summary about all these assets, plus where you can find additional information for your better preparation.

 

SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud

This certification exam validates that the candidate possesses the fundamental and core knowledge required of an SAP Commerce Cloud Business Analyst. It proves that the candidate has an overall understanding and skills to participate as a member of a project team in a mentored role. The certification is recommended as an entry level qualification. 

How to prepare: The certification question are based on the Business User Instructor-Lead Training, which is the recommended way for preparation. In addition to this, learners can also check this excellent study guide, which provides documentation links from the exam topic areas.

 

SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud 

This certification verifies that the candidate possesses the required level of skills to work effectively with the SAP Commerce Cloud Suite. It requires candidates to demonstrate technical skills and understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud. Three years of practical experience on SAP Commerce Cloud is required. 

How to prepare: The following Instructor-Lead classes are recommended for better preparation:

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1 
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2 

In addition to this, a study guide has been posted on this blog post.

 

Stay Certified

A Stay Certified program ensures you aren't left behind as SAP Commerce Cloud technology advances. It provides you the opportunity to familiarize yourself with the latest updates and product changes, and keep your certification valid. The Stay Certified program contains:

  • An online course which contains all the information about the updates, changes or SAP Commerce Cloud new features. This course is your preparation material.
  • An assessment which checks your knowledge on the latest features. When you feel comfortable with the material, you can take the assessment. If you pass it successfully, your certification will be valid for one year.

For SAP Commerce Cloud solution, we have released 2 Stay Certified programs:

  • Stay Certified for Business Users 
  • Stay Certified for Developers 

Note: The Stay Certified offer is for subscribed learners on learning.sap.com platform.

entry.title: Re: Create on-brand navigation experiences in SAP Build Apps using custom navigation
entry.content:
Hi @marihirvi thanks for the explanation :). I was trying to replicate your demo and I was wondering if what you presented here is an in-app navigation or just links between pages ?

Thanks !

entry.title: Re: Migrating SAP HANA Cloud Calc Views to SAP Data Sphere
entry.content:
Hi @ChintS,

It is my understanding that direct migration from HANA Cloud (CALC View) to Datasphere is not currently feasible. However, there are options available to address this need.

  • ECC > SLT > HANA Cloud (CALC View) > Datasphere > SAC
    You may establish a connection to HANA Cloud to observe the appearance of CALC Views in Datasphere. If there is a feature that promises remote tables, it may be worth considering, especially if you are using HANA Cloud.
  • ECC > Datasphere > SAC
    To streamline your landscape, consider simplifying it. You can connect directly to ECC from Datasphere and bring data into Datasphere. Then, you can rebuild HANA Cloud CALC Views as Datasphere views and analytics models. Datasphere and SAC will work together efficiently.
  • When are you planning to upgrade your ECC to S/4HANA? If you do, you can then use S/4HANA, which will offer additional advantages, such as the ability to use CDS Views with Datasphere.

The objective is to transfer data between these locations. To streamline this process, it would be advisable to eliminate SLT and HANA Cloud, a decision that will yield positive results in the long run.

I hope this contribution proves useful.

Thanks,
Tuncay Karaca

entry.title: Re: SAP Group reporting : copying an FS Item Value to ano...
entry.content:
Hello,

If I understand your requirement correctly, you want to copy data from one FS item to another FS item, either within the same version or to a different version. In this case, you cannot use the delivered copy function (CXCK). CXCK will only move the data to the same account from one version to another version.

To meet your requirements, we recommend leveraging the GRDC - Copy Scenarios. In this concept, GRDC will read the data from the ACDOCU table and post it in the ACDOC table. In the mapping file, you can specify the source FS item and target FS items.

In this scenario, there is currently one gap: the records will not retain the same document type or posting level.

entry.title: External Supplier review/approval in Service Entry Sheet (SES) workflow
entry.content:
Hello community members,
I have a query related to a specific business use case and need your input.

The buyer will create a PR request for lab and when lab submits their SES (Service Entry Sheet), we need an external Supplier to review/confirm/pre-approve the SES before it is sent to buyer for final approval.

How can this be configured in SES approval workflow ?

entry.title: Re: PS WBS Settings
entry.content: Check your PS configuration for the field status related to the Project Profile you are using.  That is where fields can be set to Display only.

entry.title: Re: Migrating SAP HANA Cloud Calc Views to SAP Data Sphere
entry.content: Thank you. Our plan is to eliminate HANA Cloud after activating Datasphere. We have hundreds of HANA Cloud Clac views, so redesigning is our last option. Also, does Datasphere support live connectivity with ECC?

entry.title: Starting a career in SAP finance
entry.content:
I have started taking some courses in e-learning, but the learning classes are too expensive. 

Do I have to take TS4F01 and TS4F02 in addition to the e-learning courses to pass the certification exam or are the e-learning courses enough? 

Can someone advise on alternative training classes that are less expensive?

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting: Flexible upload with LC key same as...
entry.content:
Hello,

If you have the GRDC platform enabled, you can leverage the CSV file import functionality to address your requirements. This approach allows you to handle both LC (local currency) and GC (group currency) values independently.

In this scenario, you can import LC and GC values separately, ensuring flexibility in managing the data. Unlike some other methods ( Flexible upload)  the LC value will not be automatically copied to the GC value in this approach. This distinction helps maintain accurate and specific currency data for each context.

entry.title: Batch Production Date as the Basic Start Date of a Production Order in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,
I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have a requirement related to batch production dates. Specifically, I need to configure the system so that the batch production date is automatically set to align with the basic start date of a production order.

This requirement arises because we often need to perform tasks like printing labels or documentation in advance of the goods receipt (GR). To achieve this, the production date must already be assigned to the batch before the GR step.

In previous on-premise setups, I was able to handle similar requirements, but I’m unsure how to achieve this in the public cloud environment, where configuration and customization are more restricted.

Is there a standard way or workaround to set the production date in the batch earlier (e.g., at order creation or operation confirmation) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Any insights on leveraging standard functionality, extensibility, or output management to handle this scenario would be appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to perform Quarterly consolidations in SAP S/4HAN...
entry.content:
Hello,

At this time, quarterly consolidation is not available in Group Reporting. The Group Close team currently runs the consolidation by period. We recommend reviewing the roadmap for any updates or future enhancements regarding this functionality.

As a workaround, we advise users to perform the consolidation manually every quarter by running it for three periods (e.g., consolidating the data for all periods within the quarter). This approach ensures that quarterly reporting requirements are met while the system continues to operate on a periodic consolidation basis.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan vs Item
entry.content:
hello @KerryOnn ,

If you want to track the maintenance history and maintenance cost of each equipment individually in a standard SAP Report then you need to have a separate maintenance item for each equipment. Assign the item to the maintenance plan. Please note that all the maintenance items will have same maintenance cycle therefore all the orders for 20 equipment will trigger on same day.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello Community,

SAP Note 3556441 (TR: New York Healthcare Worker Bonus in W-2/W-2c Forms Box 14 and Electronic Files) has been released.

Thank you,
Carlos

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan vs Item
entry.content: Yes, I need that tracking, though, obviously there is not enough technicians in the world to do that many machines on the same day. How can I have them stagger one every other day?

entry.title: Filter to get employee data till last week
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using the below filter in EmpJob Odata to get a snapshot of last week's employee file, it's not a delta but should get details till date 

But is my filter correct to compare endDate with lastWeekEndDate as its related to endDate(termination date) of Employee?

$filter=emplStatusNav/externalCode ne 'O' or emplStatusNav/externalCode ne 'R' or emplStatusNav/externalCode ne 'RNS' or emplStatusNav/externalCode ne 'T'
and employmentNav/hiringNotCompleted eq false and endDate lt datetime'${property.lastWeekEndDate}'

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan vs Item
entry.content: Also, and furthermore... I believe I want an item for each I want tracked each, but, I would want a plan for each for them to have their own monthly start day, am I correct?

entry.title: convert mp4 files into transcript
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on automation, if someone has some suggestions it will be of great help.
I have to automate the process to create session summary for meeting recordings. Currently, I am manually downloading the transcripts from SharePoint and using SAP AI launchpad to create session summary from transcript.
Below are some queries regarding this:
Is there anyway to automatically download meeting recording transcript from SharePoint location to local location?
If not, is there any tool in SAP that I can use to convert the .mp4 file into transcript?

entry.title: Re: NetWeaver RFC SDK: moving from Python to C
entry.content:
Most likely not, as Sebastian commented:

We haven’t been able to properly transfer the ownership to new maintainers. The reasons for that are mainly resource constraints on our side as well as the fact that we currently cannot provide the necessary prerequisites for a successful restart (e.g. access to the RFC SDK under an open-source license, backend test systems etc.).

entry.title: Re: Best Practices to Deploy MDK Application in Cloud Fou...
entry.content: Thank you Bill. Now it's all clear. I simply export/import the app from quality to production adjusting the destinations. I mistakenly thought that the Deploy to Mobile Service, by selecting the Cloud Foundry organization, would generate objects in the Cloud Foundry itself, but instead everything happens in the Mobile Service. The objects I saw in CF definitely belong to the Web App Deploy.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan vs Item
entry.content: In such a case you need to create separate maintenance plan for each equipment with different start date. This will create maintenance orders at different dates.

entry.title: Re: Create Subscription Quote Based On a Specific Configu...
entry.content: Deepak, Enabled the CONFIGDATE and able to pass the required date to launch the required product configuration. I am able to create the subscription order as expected. Initially though the CONFIG date is for Multi Level or Nested KMAT product configuration with Bill of Materials but it worked as expected. Thanks for your time in looking at the raised query and providing the guidance to resolve the issue.

entry.title: Re: Beginner Steps in Advanced SAP TM - Milk Run Scenario
entry.content: Is it possible to pick again from the location where one of the MILK-RUN stops I have already visited? For example, if I am going from A->B->C-D, can I return to "B" again?

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one attempt for SAP certificatio...
entry.content:
There is some problem. My payment went through without going to the shopping cart, but I did not see anything on my orders for at least 2 days. Then I could see an order but the status was "Booking in Progress". I think we cannot book the exam unless the status changed to "Booking Confirmed". Every one struggling to book certifications. It used to be so easy. This info may help you. 

My old certifications are not seen on my profile as well. I wrote to SAP partners and they replied that I have to take those certifications again, but never answered about linking them to my profile.  It's very frustrating and disappointing.

entry.title: Re: Which tools do you use for CSV files?
entry.content:
Hi @formulaire,

I'm currently testing out a few tools with which I can edit CSV files without using MS Excel, OpenOffice Calc, etc...

One that I find very handy is awk(1)  it allows me to create reusable scripts for repetitive tasks.
Unfortunately it isn't necessarily suitable for someone without programming knowledge.

Best regards,
Andy

entry.title: Don't Miss Our Expert Live Sessions with an SAP Sales Cloud V2 Expert – Feb 3-5, 2025
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,


The SAP Product Learning team is excited to announce a series of live sessions focused on our new product, SAP Sales Cloud V2. These sessions will take place over three 1-hour segments, led by a product expert, and are designed to provide a comprehensive understanding of SAP Sales Cloud V2's functionality and configuration.
During these sessions, we will offer valuable insights into the latest features and provide practical knowledge to help you maximize the benefits of this new product. Whether you're already familiar with SAP Sales Cloud V2 or considering its integration into your operations, these sessions will deliver the in-depth information needed to enhance your proficiency. This is also a fantastic opportunity to learn directly from an expert and ask questions in real-time.
As many of you know, SAP Sales Cloud V2 offers a wide range of features aimed at improving your sales processes. It is a cloud-native solution designed to boost productivity, manage customer relationships more effectively, and gain better insights from your data.
Don't miss out—secure your spot by signing up. We look forward to seeing you there!


Note: These sessions require a login with learning.sap.com or an SAP Learning Hub License.
Click here to register

SAP Sales Cloud  Training Customer Experience

entry.title: Prepare for SAP Sales Cloud Certification: Live Expert Sessions on Feb 11-12, 2025
entry.content:
Hello learners!

Are you looking to get certified in SAP Sales Cloud?  Here is the expert live session series for just that!

In this 2 day Live Session series, you get to learn and interact with our product expert who will be presenting the various capabilities this solution provides. This will help you with certification readiness as well, for SAP Certified Application Associate Consultant certification (C_C4H410). Each session is for 2 hrs.

(Note: This is for the Sales Cloud NGAP version, not Sales Cloud Version 2)

This live session is relevant for Administrators, Consultants, Business users or anyone interested in learning about SAP Sales Cloud.

You can register and book this session here: 

(Please Note:  You must have a subscription and  login with your S-user ID on SAP Learning Hub.)

Save your seat now! 

Happy learning!

SAP Sales Cloud

entry.title: WFA on HANA enablement issue
entry.content:
Hi,

@YSE-1 I had a bit of a trouble with getting WFA on HANA up and running for a customer - basically they did not have licenses yet, went and bought them. SAP confirms licenses are in place. Honestly I have never been involved in licensing topic, whenever I started a project the steps I would take is sort out RBP to make sure I have access to Data Factory, upload template, open support ticket for Measure Views and get on with the configuration.

Now, in this case, after SAP confirmed the licenses are in place, I couldn t find the permission for Data Factory in either Analytics permissions - Role Maintenance or RBP (manage WFA switches ON). 

I went into Provisioning and the Customer has WFA license checkbox was not enabled, however, I simply tried to mark the checkbox as per KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/0002527309 

NOTE: I have never marked that checkbox on my own, I always assumed it s automatic even though it is not grayed out like the other options below it.

Then I got the Data Factory to appear in the instance, went on with importing the template and immediately got the following error:

Strange, but then I went to check validation and found:

 

Still strange, since it s just the template and never had issues with it, went to the dimensions page - editing the dimension gives this error:

Also, trying to edit the SQL, clicking validate it also throws an error...it s the standard generic code and should work but well...

So I decided to just delete the 2 dimensions with the error and run the cube again.

After deleting them and getting back to Data Factory:

I have closed everything down and tried again back from importing a fresh template, all steps went exactly the same, so I am stuck.

Would you please be so kind to have a look into this?

Company ID: S001955610T1

Many thanks in advance!

Kind regards,

Alex

entry.title: Re: Step By Step Guide to Transport Table Contents from One Environment to Other in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Unless I misunderstood what should be the implementation of the ABAP program, method 3 described is not copying the table content from one environment to another, but instead copying content between two tables (same environment).

Thanks for your contribution though.

In addition to the methods you described, it is also possible to copy table entries via R3trans at OS level.
To do that, two control files have to be created (export_<table>.ctl and import_<table>.ctl) with respective content/format:
------------------------
export
client=<source_client>
file = ‘/usr/sap/trans/data/<TABLE_NAME>.dat’
select * from <TABLE>
------------------------

------------------------
import
file = ‘/usr/sap/trans/data/<TABLE>.dat’
client=<target_client>
------------------------

Then run R3trans:
For export: R3trans -w export_<table>.log export_<table>.ctl
And import: R3trans -w import_<table>.log import_<table>.ctl

entry.title: RMPS_SET_SUBSTITUTE equivalent in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there an equivalent app in Public cloud for transaction RMPS_SET_SUBSTITUTE?

Regards,

 

EDIT: FOUND IT

WORKFLOW ADMINISTRATOR APP

Then Set Substitute option.

entry.title: Re: prevent transport owner from transporting to production

Have you considered implementing QA steps? It is not going to achieve the exactly same, but it might be an alternative to what your client wants.

entry.title: Re: Unable to register SAP Desktop Agent 3

entry.title: ServerCrash
entry.content:
Dear expert，

The user reported that they were unable to access the server. I used sapcontrol - nr 01- function VNet cessList on the Netweaver Java application server to check the instance running status, and the textstatus of jstart showed "Some Processes running" I found the file in the attachment in the server directory/sapmnt/SID/golbal/sapcontrol/snapshots.

I found a compressed file in the directory, which contains a description.txt file and a log directory. The content of description.txt is 'Servercrash '. I found the jstart file in the log directory and it showed below:

<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] Thu Jan 9 10:53:49 2025</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** LOG => Send SIGINT to ICM (pid 16077).</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** LOG => Process icm stopping (pid 16077).</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** LOG => Instance state is "Some processes running" (RUNNING @ 2, INACTIVE).</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** LOG => Signal 17 SIGCHLD.</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** WARNING => process ICM (pid 16077) killed (signal 11). [sfuxlib.hpp 854]</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** LOG => Process icm died (pid 16077).</item>
<item>F [Thr 140016985638784] *** WARNING => Node icm failed: result -1, exit code 139. [sfxxnode.hpp 1036]</item>

I checked the memory, but found that it was only half used. What else can I check?

entry.title: Re: Higher priority work order reservation should not be...
entry.content:
Hello @preethialevarthi ,

In such situation you can do an enhancement in work order. If the priority is low and user wants to block the material specifically for the order then at the time of saving the order the material will automatically transferred to a virtual storage location (HARD). Now this storage location will be copied in ur order under component tab. The same storage location will populate automatically at the time of goods issue. The other order will not be able to issue it because it has the normal storage location in order.

So no need to put any HARD indicator on reservation. Transfer your material to HARD location where others cannot issue it. In the maintenance order component tab the storage location should be grey and at the time of MIGO the storage location cannot be selected as HARD manually. Otherwise person with storage location as normal can manually change the storage location and try to get material from HARD storage location.

Hope that it will help.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Context Attributes for SAP Released Event Types
entry.content:
Hi @SafaBahoosh ,

thanks for this detailed article!

When trying to apply this, a few questions come to my mind.

Firstly, the example you describe about the sales order seems not to apply to my system (S4Hana onprem 2023 as you mentioned).
For example, the abstract entity D_SalesOrderCreated is missing the "allowExtension" metadata tag in my case. Is there anything that needs to be done/enabled in advance?

More importantly though, I noticed that several central business objects (i.e. Product, BusinessPartner, ProductionOrder) seem to not be suitable to the approach you are describing.
On the example of product. Business Accelerator Hub refers to I_PRODUCTTP_2. Not only is this behavior definition not extensible also there no event definitions inside. Using the relation explorer in eclipse, one can find R_PRODUCTTP (not extensible) which again refers to D_ProductCreates (not extensible) as event.

In summary this seems to me that there is no way to create outbound filters for Product at all. Can you elaborate if this is the case or what I am missing?

entry.title: Re: Enhanced Shortcut Scenario SAP TM
entry.content: I followed the exact steps but still unable to generate the FO

entry.title: @sap/xsodata direct consumption doesnt work with SAP Business One SCHEMA
entry.content:
Dear all

i'm trying to directly use @SAP/xsodata for SAP Business ONE SCHEMA browsing.

I've copied the examples in the docs directory but, when i'm trying to consume the service, i got a weird error:

IS_COLUMN_VIEW is not defined.

Investigating further seems that you must change line 483 in metadataReader.js to 

let schema = entityType.schema || context.defaultSchema, sql = 'select IS_COLUMN_TABLE as "IS_COLUMN_TYPE" from SYS.TABLES where SCHEMA_NAME = ? AND TABLE_NAME = ? ' + 'union select IS_COLUMN_VIEW as "IS_COLUMN_TYPE" from SYS.VIEWS where SCHEMA_NAME = ? AND VIEW_NAME = ?', tableName = entityType.tableName, parameters = [schema, tableName, schema, tableName];​I.E. you MUST add SYS. to TABLES and VIEWS internal tables.

Is that a bug, it's by design or am i missing something?

 

Thanks in advance

Nicola

entry.title: Re: Cannot Install Crystal reports 2016 on Windows 11
entry.content:
Given the steps you've taken, here are some additional suggestions to help resolve the installation issue:


# Potential Solutions:

1. *Compatibility Mode*: Right-click the setup.exe file, select Properties, and under the Compatibility tab, check "Run this program in compatibility mode for" and select Windows 8 or Windows 7.
2. *Disable Windows Defender*: Temporarily disable Windows Defender to ensure it's not interfering with the installation.
3. *Install in a different location*: Try installing Crystal Reports 2016 in a different location, such as C:\CR2016, instead of the default Program Files (x86) directory.
4. *Check for conflicting applications*: Ensure that no other applications are running that might be conflicting with the installation, such as other SAP or BusinessObjects applications.
5. *Install the latest Windows updates*: Ensure your Windows 11 laptop is up-to-date with the latest patches and updates.
6. *Try a clean boot*: Perform a clean boot to start Windows with a minimal set of drivers and startup programs.
7. *Check the Event Viewer*: Review the Event Viewer logs for any errors related to the installation.
8. *Contact SAP Support*: Reach out to SAP Support for further assistance, as they may be able to provide more specific guidance or troubleshooting steps.

# Additional Questions:

1. Have you tried installing Crystal Reports 2016 on another Windows 11 machine to rule out any laptop-specific issues?
2. Are there any specific error messages or codes that appear during the installation process?
3. Have you checked the system requirements for Crystal Reports 2016 to ensure your laptop meets the minimum requirements?

By trying these additional steps and providing more information, we can work together to resolve the installation issue.

entry.title: Streamlining Warehouse Operations with Unified Delivery Note Templates in SAP Business Network
entry.content:
Using SAP Business Network, you can streamline warehouse operations by providing suppliers with a unified delivery note template to attach to shipped goods.

 

Suppliers can access the template while creating or viewing an advance shipping notice, which later becomes an inbound delivery in SAP. Here's how it appears to suppliers:

Templates can range from simple formats like this:

To more detailed, line-item-oriented designs:

Templates can also use QR codes instead of barcodes, provided your scanning system supports it. QR codes add an extra layer of security.

To implement this feature, SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration licenses include it in the deployment plan. The SAP Deployment team will handle the setup, starting with a requirements gathering session involving SAP Buyer integrator. After the template is built, basic testing ensures it meets your needs. Once finalized, templates are managed at the supplier group level.

The way you assign templates to suppliers is the same for test and production. Often customers would like to default a single template to all suppliers moving forward in production—that is possible in production. Unfortunately, you need to keep adding new suppliers as members of the group as part of your business-as-usual operations.

 

Notes from Past Project Experience:

  • Templates can also be configured for SBN Commerce Automation projects, though suppliers not involved in manufacturing are typically excluded.
  • In rare cases, templates have been used for standard account suppliers, though this can be challenging.

entry.title: FBL*H transactions - OOM Dump(STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG) and analysis
entry.content:
Hi! How are you? 

When users execute FB(1-3-5)H operations, the database triggers an Out of Memory (OOM) dump.

OOM dump, memory used is over 300GB and is stopped Allocation Type: STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG.

Error Text: "cannot allocate enough memory: [9] Memory allocation failed exception 1000002: Allocation failed $failure_type$=STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG $failure_flag$= $size$=18867620288 $name$=TranslationTable $type$=pool $inuse_count$=73513 $allocated_size$=463669603848 $alignment$=8 ,Exception in executor 30040 while executing pop 68"

I can run the query in "DBACOCKPIT > Expensive Statements" via SQL editor without any problems.

When the user runs the same query hours later, it works fine 🙂

SAP provided OSS note 3442793, and it was applied successfully and HDBVIEWS transaction code was executed without errors.

 
Additionally, performance-related notes 3303070 and 3193112 were also applied.

The customer needs a quick solution. I need help pls!!

Software Component Version: S4CORE 104

entry.title: Re: S/4 public cloud RFC call
entry.content:
Hi Marty,

ony usecase that I just came around is the maybe missing support for Variant Configuration in the OData API. Only the new OData v4 Sales Order API supports Variant Configuration. If you need Variant Configuration also for Inquiries or Sales Quotations then the only option is the BAPI_INQUIRY_CREATEFROMDATA2 or BAPI_QUOTATION_CREATEFROMDATA2.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: S/4 public cloud RFC call
entry.content:
Hi @DLange,

please check out the blog post Calling BAPIs in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for the possible options. Unfortunately the SAP Note/KBA 2447593 has been removed.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Fundamental concepts for any SAP FICO consultant
entry.content:
Organizational Structure:

✔ Client: The highest level in the SAP system. It represents an independent organizational unit (e.g., a corporation). All other organizational units are assigned to a client.

✔Company Code: The central organizational unit in Financial Accounting (FI). It's an independent legal entity for which complete financial statements can be created (balance sheet, income statement).

✔Controlling Area: The central organizational unit in Controlling (CO). It's used for cost accounting and internal reporting. One or more company codes can be assigned to a controlling area. This is where cost and revenue flow.

✔Business Area: An organizational unit used for internal reporting based on different lines of business or geographical locations within a company code. It's less commonly used in modern SAP implementations, often replaced by profit centers or segments.

Financial Accounting (FI):

🌡 Chart of Accounts (COA): A list of all general ledger (G/L) accounts used by one or more company codes. It defines the structure and numbering of G/L accounts.

🌡 Account Groups: Control the fields that are required, optional, suppressed, or displayed when creating or changing G/L master records. They are defined at the chart of accounts level.

🌡 Fiscal Year Variant: Defines the start and end of the fiscal year and the number of posting periods. It's assigned to the company code.

🌡 Document Types: Classify accounting documents (e.g., invoice, payment, journal entry). They control the number range for documents and which account types can be posted to.

🌡 Posting Keys: Two-digit keys that determine the account type (e.g., G/L account, customer, vendor, asset), the debit/credit indicator, and other posting-relevant information.

🌡 Number Ranges: Define the numbering system for various objects, such as accounting documents, assets, and materials. They prevent duplicate numbers.

🌡 House Banks: The banks with which a company has accounts. Bank master data is maintained here, including account numbers, bank keys, and other relevant information for payment processing.

🌡 Ledger: In S4HANA, ledgers are the central repository for accounting data. The leading ledger (usually 0L) is used for legal reporting, while other ledgers (non-leading ledgers) can be used for parallel accounting according to different accounting principles (e.g., IFRS, US GAAP).

Asset Accounting (AA):

🧯Chart of Depreciation: A list of all depreciation areas used by one or more company codes. It defines the depreciation methods and parameters.

🧯Asset Class: A grouping of assets with similar characteristics (e.g., buildings, machinery, vehicles). It determines the default account assignments, depreciation keys, and other asset-related information.

🧯 Depreciation Area: Represents different valuation methods for assets (e.g., book depreciation, tax depreciation). Each asset can have multiple depreciation areas.

Materials Management (MM) / Controlling (CO) Integration:

✋Valuation Class: Links materials to G/L accounts in FI. It determines which accounts are debited or credited during goods movements.

✋ Material Ledger: In S4HANA, the Material Ledger is mandatory. It collects actual costs for materials and allows for different inventory valuation methods (e.g., moving average price, standard price). It is crucial for parallel valuation and actual costing.


Key Relationships:

Company codes are assigned to a client and a controlling area.

A chart of accounts is assigned to company codes.

A fiscal year variant is assigned to company codes.

A chart of depreciation is assigned to company codes.

Valuation classes link materials in MM to G/L accounts in FI (via account determination).

Understanding these relationships is crucial for configuring and troubleshooting SAP FICO. They represent the foundation of how financial and management accounting data is organized and processed within the system.

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_sap-activity-7281767669370949632-Wln4?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Consumption/Adjusting the usage Cycle to PRT in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,     

We need solution for below scenario in Public cloud.                                                                         The customer requires  PRT be utilize for 5 production cycles. After the 5th order, the PRT will go for Rework/Consumed and replaced with a new PRT for the 6th production cycle.                                          Is it OKAY to use Equipment PRT in this case??                                                                                   If Yes  For testing,  We have already created the Equipment PRT, along with a counter and a maintenance plan, and assigned it to the routing of the finished goods (FG) material. After confirming the production order, report is generating(Tcode- IK17) for the quantity of equipment consumed.            However, how can we get the report on basis of PRT used in No. of production orders and its restriction beyond 5th production order.

Is there any provision which suits better for above case except PRT                                       Please help

entry.title: Re: Tax classification not fully maintained
entry.content:

Can you please guide how you resolved the issue we are facing same issue while uploading material master using migration cockpit please revert if possible it's bit urgent.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Not able to upload values of UDF via DTW SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi,

To add UDFs to the Excel templates in DTW, follow these steps:

1- Login to DTW.
2- Go to Templates and select Customize Template.
3- Click on Select Object.
4- In your case, navigate to Inventory > Inventory Transactions > Goods Receipt.
5- Highlight Document and expand the drop-down list by clicking the + symbol to check if the UDFs you created appear in the list.
6- Once confirmed, right-click on Document and select Create Template for Structure.
This will generate a new template with the UDFs included, allowing you to populate and upload the required data.

Best regards,
Si mohamed ANDDAM

entry.content:
Hi Prashant,

Please raise incident in component GRC-UDS-DO.

Regards

Kulvendra

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Semantic Onboarding - Dimensions / Text View - Usability
entry.content: Hey Nico :). Only BW objects from BW/4 can be "onboarded" directly. From CDS Views, you just use this as a template - like BW content, which you either copied or modified to your needs, but never used out-of-the-box. You can use the replication flow to create the target table proposal and before you deploy, you alter the table. Cheers, Martin

entry.title: Re: Step By Step Guide to Transport Table Contents from One Environment to Other in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Download and Upload High Volume

  • Use Case: This method is suitable for transferring large amounts of data 
  • Steps:
    1. Download Data:
      • Read data from the source table using an ABAP program.
      • Export the data to a file (e.g., CSV, XML).
    2. Upload Data:
      • Read the data from the file.
      • Import the data into the target table using an ABAP program.
  • Considerations:
    • This method is suitable for high-volume data transfers.
    • Data formatting and conversion might be required during the import and export process.
    • Ensure data integrity and consistency during the transfer.
    • Consider using appropriate data handling techniques (e.g., batch processing) to avoid performance issues.

If you need to copy table content between two different SAP systems, you could also use different methods, such as:

  • SLD (System Landscape Directory): This tool can be used to transport data between different systems in an SAP landscape.
  • File-based transfer: Export data from the source system to a file, and then import it into the target system. This method requires careful data formatting and handling.
  • Third-party tools: Several third-party tools are available for data migration between SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Remote table not reflecting measures properly in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

In your CDS view you have to join with the TCURX and alter the value accordingly. 

These currencies have only 1 decimal place, instead of 3. This causes issues in change logs, etc. for example. You have to catch this in the CDS, or create a DB view and then redirect your CDS to that DB view. 

Cheers,

Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Voice: Devtoberfest, New Features, Popular Content, and More
entry.content: Where can I find the latest SAP Community Voice? It is published once a month, isn't it?

entry.title: Re: Load BW On Hana DSO/CUBE into DataSphere( Configuration)
entry.content: No special config for ODP, just activate. Cubes --> hmm. OK. I used BW/4 as source only, but if SAP says so. I guess via ODP you still can load from Cubes, or exposing HANA View. Interesting however and thank you for the hint.

entry.title: Re: How to implement additional booking periods (More tha...
entry.content: Achso. Then create your own association on top of the time-tables. Based on CSV f.e. Or add the periods 13-16 via union all. And associate this new view to your model-view. This way you will have two different time-associations in the system, but the second will be for the periods 13-16. Another option is fiscal calendar, which contains these special periods used in CO.

entry.title: Light and Dark theme in SAP Analytics cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Objective: Implement theme switching in SAP Analytics Cloud using an optimized approach.

Solution: It is possible to implement by using Optimised Design Experience (ODE).

 

These are the overview of the steps to achieve switching themes in SAP SAC.

  1. Script variable
  2. Theme preferences
  3. Switch implement and onChange script
  4. Story onInitalization script 

 

Step 1: 

  • Create a script variable and set "light" as the default value

Step 2:

  • Create two theme preferences, one for Light and another for Dark.

It allows to define plenty of styling options for each widget, and it is much easier to use than defining CSS.

 

  • To achieve the Light theme, the Background color for each widget should be Light grey color, select light theme as DEFAULT.
  • the Dark theme, the Background color for each widget should be Light black color.

Step 3:

  • Create a switch component by Insert -> Filters/Controls -> Switch

  • Set switch value data source type as "Script variables"
  • Provide the ID Name for a switch component as "Switch_2"

Step 4:

  • Once done with theme preferences, Now it is time to Script the logic
  • It is necessary to script logic in switch's fx onChange() and story's onInitalization()

  • Now in switch's fx onChange() , The script toggles between light and dark themes based on the current ThemeMode.

    1. If the theme is dark:

      • Switch to the light theme by using (Application.setTheme()). 
      • Application.setTheme()Click the Ctrl+space bar. It will allow you to choose the corresponding theme preferences automatically and fetch the theme ID.
      • I used Icons for light and dark theme, Initially Hide DarkIcon and show LightIcon.

    2. Otherwise (theme is light):

      • Switch to the dark theme.
      • Hide LightIcon and show DarkIcon.

    This ensures dynamic theme and icon adjustments based on user interaction.

  • Code:
  • if ( ThemeMode === "dark" )
    {
                    ThemeMode = "light";
                    Application.setTheme("369CEA6B9DC57C13BF5FA65BB646871C");
    DarkIcon.setVisible(false);
    LightIcon.setVisible(true);
     
    }
    else{
    Application.setTheme("DE00DBCBBDA02C61A64EF48759CF3E43");
                    ThemeMode = "dark";
    LightIcon.setVisible(false);
    DarkIcon.setVisible(true);
     
     
                    }
     

  • Now in the Story's fx, select onInitialization() and The script initializes LightIcon as visible and DarkIcon as hidden. It then toggles their visibility based on the ON/OFF state of Switch_2.

  • Code
DarkIcon.setVisible(false);
LightIcon.setVisible(true);
 
 
if( Switch_2.isOn() === true){
DarkIcon.setVisible(true);
LightIcon.setVisible(false);
} else{
LightIcon.setVisible(true);  
DarkIcon.setVisible(false);
}

Dont forget to Save the story and view !

Hurraayyyy successfully implemented the Light and Dark theme in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) 😎.
